other_observers: None.; I am not the original finder. Dan Maynard told me about the bird.
specific_location: East end of cottonwood grove on the Thompson Ranch.
description: I first noticed the small passerine, roughly empid or wood-pewee in size, as it fluttered above a 30-foot cottonwood tree. The bird was bi-colored - a bright red on crown and its entire underparts from chin to belly, and an earthy brown on wings, back, tail, and large ocular stripe on the side of its head.Behaviors: I first noticed it briefly fluttering just above the treetop of a 30-foot cottonwood as if it were displaying. The flycatcher landed on the tree top for less than a minute before flying out of site into a grove of trees. I did not see or hear the bird again during my visit.Call: None heard.Plumage: Alternate
